message:
docs: eval dashboard implementation plan — 9 tasks

Phase 1: FastAPI server, data layer, trajectory parser, markdown
renderer, API routes, frontend SPA, and deployment to magic-kingdom.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,1964 @@ +# Eval Dashboard Implementation Plan + +> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. + +**Goal:** Build a live FastAPI dashboard for eval results with trajectory-based transcript viewing and a markdown-first API. + +**Architecture:** Single FastAPI process reads eval data from disk (harbor job directories). Returns markdown by default, JSON when the frontend requests it via `Accept: application/json`. Vanilla JS SPA frontend — no build step, no npm. + +**Tech Stack:** Python 3.12, FastAPI, uvicorn. Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS for frontend. pytest for tests. + +**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-03-01-eval-dashboard-design.md` + +--- + +### Task 1: Project skeleton and test fixtures + +Set up the directory structure, install dependencies, create test fixtures, and verify the skeleton runs. + +**Files:** +- Create: `tools/dashboard/__init__.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/server.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/data.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/trajectory.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/markdown_render.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/static/index.html` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/static/style.css` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/static/app.js` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/test_data.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/test_trajectory.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/test_markdown.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/test_server.py` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/conftest.py` + +**Step 1: Create the directory structure** + +```bash +mkdir -p tools/dashboard/static +touch tools/dashboard/__init__.py +``` + +**Step 2: Create conftest.py with test fixtures** + +The fixtures create a temporary harbor-style job directory with two tasks (one pass, one fail), each with transcript JSONL files. This is the foundation for all tests. + +```python +"""Shared test fixtures for dashboard tests.""" + +import json +import os +import pytest + + +@pytest.fixture +def harbor_job_dir(tmp_path): + """Create a harbor-style job directory with two tasks. + + Structure: + full-test/full-test/ + build-widget__abc123/ + verifier/reward.txt (1.0 - pass) + agent/serf-state/sessions/sess-main.transcript.jsonl + agent/serf-state/sessions/sess-reviewer.transcript.jsonl + agent/serf-state/api.jsonl + agent/command-0/stdout.txt + result.json + fix-bug__def456/ + verifier/reward.txt (0.0 - fail) + agent/serf-state/sessions/sess-main2.transcript.jsonl + agent/command-0/stdout.txt + result.json + """ + job_root = tmp_path / "full-test" / "full-test" + + # --- Task 1: build-widget (PASS) --- + t1 = job_root / "build-widget__abc123" + _make_task(t1, reward=1.0, transcript_entries=_passing_transcript()) + + # --- Task 2: fix-bug (FAIL, wrong answer) --- + t2 = job_root / "fix-bug__def456" + _make_task(t2, reward=0.0, transcript_entries=_failing_transcript(), + agent_stdout="[submit_result] submitted\n") + + return tmp_path / "full-test" + + +def _make_task(task_dir, reward, transcript_entries, agent_stdout=""): + """Create a single task directory with required files.""" + # Verifier + verifier = task_dir / "verifier" + verifier.mkdir(parents=True) + (verifier / "reward.txt").write_text(str(reward)) + if reward < 1.0: + (verifier / "test-stdout.txt").write_text( + "FAIL: test_widget (expected 42, got 0)\n" + ) + + # Sessions + sessions = task_dir / "agent" / "serf-state" / "sessions" + sessions.mkdir(parents=True) + + # Write transcript + tf = sessions / "sess-main.transcript.jsonl" + lines = [json.dumps(e) for e in transcript_entries] + tf.write_text("\n".join(lines) + "\n") + + # Agent stdout + cmd_dir = task_dir / "agent" / "command-0" + cmd_dir.mkdir(parents=True) + (cmd_dir / "stdout.txt").write_text(agent_stdout) + + # result.json + (task_dir / "result.json").write_text(json.dumps({ + "config": {"model": "gpt-5.3-codex"}, + })) + + +def _passing_transcript(): + """Transcript entries for a passing task: explore, edit, exec, submit.""" + return [ + {"kind": "header", "format_version": 1, "session_id": "sess-main", + "created_at": "2026-03-01T12:00:00Z", "model": "gpt-5.3-codex", + "profile_id": "openai", "depth": 0}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 0, "turn": { + "kind": "USER_INPUT", + "message": {"role": "user", "content": [ + {"kind": "text", "text": "Build a widget that returns 42."} + ]}, + "timestamp": "2026-03-01T12:00:01Z", + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 1, "turn": { + "kind": "ASSISTANT", + "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [ + {"kind": "text", "text": "I'll start by reading the workspace."}, + {"kind": "tool_call", "tool_call": { + "id": "tc-1", "name": "glob", + "arguments": "{\"pattern\": \"*\"}", + }}, + ]}, + "timestamp": "2026-03-01T12:00:02Z", + "usage": {"input_tokens": 500, "output_tokens": 30}, + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 2, "turn": { + "kind": "TOOL_RESULTS", + "message": {"role": "tool", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_result", "tool_result": { + "tool_call_id": "tc-1", "name": "glob", + "content": "main.py\ntest_main.py", "is_error": False, + }}, + ]}, + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 3, "turn": { + "kind": "ASSISTANT", + "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_call", "tool_call": { + "id": "tc-2", "name": "apply_patch", + "arguments": "{\"patch\": \"--- a/main.py\\n+++ b/main.py\\n@@ -1 +1 @@\\n-pass\\n+def widget(): return 42\"}", + }}, + ]}, + "timestamp": "2026-03-01T12:00:03Z", + "usage": {"input_tokens": 600, "output_tokens": 40}, + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 4, "turn": { + "kind": "TOOL_RESULTS", + "message": {"role": "tool", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_result", "tool_result": { + "tool_call_id": "tc-2", "name": "apply_patch", + "content": "Applied.", "is_error": False, + }}, + ]}, + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 5, "turn": { + "kind": "ASSISTANT", + "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_call", "tool_call": { + "id": "tc-3", "name": "shell", + "arguments": "{\"command\": \"python -c 'from main import widget; print(widget())'\"}", + }}, + ]}, + "timestamp": "2026-03-01T12:00:04Z", + "usage": {"input_tokens": 700, "output_tokens": 25}, + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 6, "turn": { + "kind": "TOOL_RESULTS", + "message": {"role": "tool", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_result", "tool_result": { + "tool_call_id": "tc-3", "name": "shell", + "content": "42", "is_error": False, + }}, + ]}, + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 7, "turn": { + "kind": "ASSISTANT", + "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_call", "tool_call": { + "id": "tc-4", "name": "communicate", + "arguments": "{\"result\": \"Widget implemented.\"}", + }}, + ]}, + "timestamp": "2026-03-01T12:00:05Z", + "usage": {"input_tokens": 750, "output_tokens": 20}, + }}, + ] + + +def _failing_transcript(): + """Transcript for a failing task: explore, wrong edit, submit.""" + return [ + {"kind": "header", "format_version": 1, "session_id": "sess-main2", + "created_at": "2026-03-01T13:00:00Z", "model": "gpt-5.3-codex", + "profile_id": "openai", "depth": 0}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 0, "turn": { + "kind": "USER_INPUT", + "message": {"role": "user", "content": [ + {"kind": "text", "text": "Fix the bug in widget."} + ]}, + "timestamp": "2026-03-01T13:00:01Z", + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 1, "turn": { + "kind": "ASSISTANT", + "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_call", "tool_call": { + "id": "tc-10", "name": "read_file", + "arguments": "{\"path\": \"main.py\"}", + }}, + ]}, + "timestamp": "2026-03-01T13:00:02Z", + "usage": {"input_tokens": 400, "output_tokens": 15}, + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 2, "turn": { + "kind": "TOOL_RESULTS", + "message": {"role": "tool", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_result", "tool_result": { + "tool_call_id": "tc-10", "name": "read_file", + "content": "def widget(): return 0", "is_error": False, + }}, + ]}, + }}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 3, "turn": { + "kind": "ASSISTANT", + "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [ + {"kind": "tool_call", "tool_call": { + "id": "tc-11", "name": "communicate", + "arguments": "{\"result\": \"Looks fine to me.\"}", + }}, + ]}, + "timestamp": "2026-03-01T13:00:03Z", + "usage": {"input_tokens": 500, "output_tokens": 10}, + }}, + ] +``` + +**Step 3: Create minimal server.py** + +```python +"""Eval dashboard server.""" + +from fastapi import FastAPI +from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles +import os + +app = FastAPI(title="Serf Eval Dashboard") + + +@app.get("/health") +def health(): + return {"status": "ok"} + + +# Serve static files last (catch-all) +static_dir = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "static") +if os.path.isdir(static_dir): + app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory=static_dir), name="static") +``` + +**Step 4: Create placeholder files** + +Create empty `data.py`, `trajectory.py`, `markdown_render.py`, and minimal `static/index.html`: + +```html + + + + + + Serf Eval Dashboard + + + +
Loading...
+ + + +``` + +Empty `style.css` and `app.js`. + +**Step 5: Verify** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard +pip install fastapi uvicorn httpx # httpx needed for TestClient +pytest conftest.py --co # verify fixtures load +python -c "from server import app; print('OK')" +``` + +**Step 6: Commit** + +```bash +git add tools/dashboard/ +git commit -m "feat(dashboard): project skeleton with test fixtures" +``` + +--- + +### Task 2: Data layer — run discovery and task reading + +Build the `RunStore` class that discovers runs from harbor job directories and reads +task results (rewards, failure categories, session counts). + +**Files:** +- Create: `tools/dashboard/data.py` +- Modify: `tools/dashboard/test_data.py` + +**Step 1: Write failing tests in test_data.py** + +```python +"""Tests for the data layer.""" + +import pytest +from data import RunStore + + +class TestRunDiscovery: + def test_discovers_run(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + runs = store.list_runs() + assert len(runs) == 1 + assert runs[0]["job_name"] == "full-test" + + def test_empty_dir(self, tmp_path): + store = RunStore([tmp_path]) + assert store.list_runs() == [] + + def test_multiple_dirs(self, harbor_job_dir, tmp_path): + # Create a second job + job2 = tmp_path / "other-run" / "other-run" + task = job2 / "some-task__xyz789" / "verifier" + task.mkdir(parents=True) + (task / "reward.txt").write_text("1.0") + # Agent dirs + sessions = job2 / "some-task__xyz789" / "agent" / "serf-state" / "sessions" + sessions.mkdir(parents=True) + + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir, tmp_path / "other-run"]) + runs = store.list_runs() + assert len(runs) == 2 + + +class TestTaskReading: + def test_list_tasks(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + tasks = store.list_tasks("full-test") + names = [t["name"] for t in tasks] + assert "build-widget" in names + assert "fix-bug" in names + + def test_task_reward(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + tasks = store.list_tasks("full-test") + by_name = {t["name"]: t for t in tasks} + assert by_name["build-widget"]["reward"] == 1.0 + assert by_name["fix-bug"]["reward"] == 0.0 + + def test_task_pass_fail(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + tasks = store.list_tasks("full-test") + by_name = {t["name"]: t for t in tasks} + assert by_name["build-widget"]["passed"] is True + assert by_name["fix-bug"]["passed"] is False + + def test_failure_category(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + tasks = store.list_tasks("full-test") + by_name = {t["name"]: t for t in tasks} + assert by_name["fix-bug"]["failure_category"] == "wrong_answer" + + def test_session_count(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + tasks = store.list_tasks("full-test") + by_name = {t["name"]: t for t in tasks} + assert by_name["build-widget"]["session_count"] >= 1 + + def test_run_summary(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + run = store.get_run("full-test") + assert run["task_count"] == 2 + assert run["pass_count"] == 1 + + def test_unknown_run(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + assert store.get_run("nonexistent") is None +``` + +**Step 2: Run tests, verify they fail** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard && pytest test_data.py -v +``` + +Expected: ImportError or AttributeError (RunStore doesn't exist yet). + +**Step 3: Implement data.py** + +```python +"""Data layer — discover runs and read task results from disk.""" + +import glob +import json +import os +import re + + +class RunStore: + """Discovers and reads eval runs from harbor job directories. + + Each data_dir should contain a subdirectory named after the job, + which contains task directories in harbor's task__hash format. + + Example: /tmp/full-mk6/full-mk6/build-cython-ext__abc123/ + """ + + def __init__(self, data_dirs: list[str]): + self._data_dirs = [str(d) for d in data_dirs] + + def _discover_jobs(self) -> dict[str, str]: + """Return {job_name: job_path} for all discovered jobs.""" + jobs = {} + for data_dir in self._data_dirs: + if not os.path.isdir(data_dir): + continue + # Harbor creates: data_dir/job_name/task__hash/ + # Check if data_dir itself contains task dirs + if self._has_task_dirs(data_dir): + name = os.path.basename(data_dir) + jobs[name] = data_dir + continue + # Otherwise scan subdirectories + for entry in os.listdir(data_dir): + sub = os.path.join(data_dir, entry) + if os.path.isdir(sub) and self._has_task_dirs(sub): + jobs[entry] = sub + return jobs + + def _has_task_dirs(self, path: str) -> bool: + """Check if a directory contains harbor task directories (name__hash).""" + try: + for entry in os.listdir(path): + if "__" in entry and os.path.isdir(os.path.join(path, entry)): + return True + except OSError: + pass + return False + + def _task_dirs(self, job_path: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]: + """Return [(task_name, task_dir_path)] for a job directory.""" + results = [] + for entry in sorted(os.listdir(job_path)): + full = os.path.join(job_path, entry) + if not os.path.isdir(full) or "__" not in entry: + continue + task_name = entry.rsplit("__", 1)[0] + results.append((task_name, full)) + return results + + def list_runs(self) -> list[dict]: + """List all discovered runs with summary info.""" + runs = [] + for job_name, job_path in sorted(self._discover_jobs().items()): + tasks = self._task_dirs(job_path) + completed = 0 + passed = 0 + for _, td in tasks: + reward = self._read_reward(td) + if reward is not None: + completed += 1 + if reward > 0: + passed += 1 + + manifest = self._read_manifest(job_path) + + runs.append({ + "job_name": job_name, + "path": job_path, + "task_count": len(tasks), + "completed": completed, + "pass_count": passed, + "status": "complete" if completed == len(tasks) else "running", + "model": manifest.get("model", ""), + "git_sha": manifest.get("git_sha", ""), + "started_at": manifest.get("started_at", ""), + }) + return runs + + def get_run(self, job_name: str) -> dict | None: + """Get detailed info for a specific run.""" + jobs = self._discover_jobs() + if job_name not in jobs: + return None + + job_path = jobs[job_name] + tasks = self.list_tasks(job_name) + manifest = self._read_manifest(job_path) + + pass_count = sum(1 for t in tasks if t["passed"]) + fail_cats = {} + for t in tasks: + cat = t.get("failure_category", "") + if cat: + fail_cats[cat] = fail_cats.get(cat, 0) + 1 + + return { + "job_name": job_name, + "path": job_path, + "task_count": len(tasks), + "pass_count": pass_count, + "failure_categories": fail_cats, + "status": "complete" if all( + t["reward"] is not None for t in tasks + ) else "running", + "model": manifest.get("model", ""), + "git_sha": manifest.get("git_sha", ""), + "started_at": manifest.get("started_at", ""), + "manifest": manifest, + } + + def list_tasks(self, job_name: str) -> list[dict]: + """List all tasks in a run with results.""" + jobs = self._discover_jobs() + if job_name not in jobs: + return [] + + job_path = jobs[job_name] + results = [] + for task_name, task_dir in self._task_dirs(job_path): + reward = self._read_reward(task_dir) + passed = reward is not None and reward > 0 + results.append({ + "name": task_name, + "reward": reward, + "passed": passed, + "failure_category": self._classify_failure(task_dir, reward), + "session_count": self._count_sessions(task_dir), + "dir": task_dir, + }) + return results + + def get_task(self, job_name: str, task_name: str) -> dict | None: + """Get a specific task's data including transcript paths.""" + jobs = self._discover_jobs() + if job_name not in jobs: + return None + + for name, task_dir in self._task_dirs(jobs[job_name]): + if name == task_name: + task = { + "name": name, + "reward": self._read_reward(task_dir), + "failure_category": self._classify_failure( + task_dir, self._read_reward(task_dir) + ), + "session_count": self._count_sessions(task_dir), + "dir": task_dir, + "transcript_files": self._find_transcripts(task_dir), + "verifier_output": self._read_file( + os.path.join(task_dir, "verifier", "test-stdout.txt") + ), + "agent_stdout": self._read_file( + os.path.join(task_dir, "agent", "command-0", "stdout.txt") + ), + } + task["passed"] = ( + task["reward"] is not None and task["reward"] > 0 + ) + return task + return None + + def _read_reward(self, task_dir: str) -> float | None: + path = os.path.join(task_dir, "verifier", "reward.txt") + if not os.path.isfile(path): + return None + try: + return float(open(path).read().strip()) + except (ValueError, OSError): + return None + + def _read_manifest(self, job_path: str) -> dict: + """Read manifest.json from the job directory or its parent.""" + for candidate in [ + os.path.join(job_path, "manifest.json"), + os.path.join(os.path.dirname(job_path), "manifest.json"), + ]: + if os.path.isfile(candidate): + try: + return json.load(open(candidate)) + except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError): + pass + return {} + + def _classify_failure(self, task_dir: str, reward: float | None) -> str: + """Classify why a task failed.""" + if reward is None: + return "running" + if reward > 0: + return "" + + # Check result.json for timeout + result_path = os.path.join(task_dir, "result.json") + if os.path.isfile(result_path): + try: + r = json.load(open(result_path)) + exc = r.get("exception_info", {}) + if exc.get("exception_type") == "AgentTimeoutError": + return "timeout" + except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError): + pass + + # Check agent stdout for submission + stdout_path = os.path.join( + task_dir, "agent", "command-0", "stdout.txt" + ) + stdout = self._read_file(stdout_path) or "" + if "[submit_result]" in stdout or "[communicate]" in stdout: + return "wrong_answer" + + if "[error]" in stdout: + return "api_error" + + return "no_submit" + + def _count_sessions(self, task_dir: str) -> int: + sessions_dir = os.path.join( + task_dir, "agent", "serf-state", "sessions" + ) + if not os.path.isdir(sessions_dir): + return 0 + return len([ + f for f in os.listdir(sessions_dir) + if f.endswith(".transcript.jsonl") + ]) + + def _find_transcripts(self, task_dir: str) -> list[str]: + sessions_dir = os.path.join( + task_dir, "agent", "serf-state", "sessions" + ) + if not os.path.isdir(sessions_dir): + return [] + return sorted( + os.path.join(sessions_dir, f) + for f in os.listdir(sessions_dir) + if f.endswith(".transcript.jsonl") + ) + + def _read_file(self, path: str) -> str | None: + if not os.path.isfile(path): + return None + try: + return open(path).read() + except OSError: + return None +``` + +**Step 4: Run tests, verify they pass** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard && pytest test_data.py -v +``` + +Expected: all pass. + +**Step 5: Commit** + +```bash +git add tools/dashboard/data.py tools/dashboard/test_data.py +git commit -m "feat(dashboard): data layer — run discovery and task reading" +``` + +--- + +### Task 3: Transcript parsing and session hierarchy + +Parse transcript JSONL files into structured sessions with parent-child relationships. + +**Files:** +- Modify: `tools/dashboard/data.py` (add `load_transcripts()`) +- Modify: `tools/dashboard/test_data.py` (add transcript tests) + +**Step 1: Write failing tests** + +Add to `test_data.py`: + +```python +class TestTranscriptLoading: + def test_loads_sessions(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + task = store.get_task("full-test", "build-widget") + sessions = store.load_transcripts(task["transcript_files"]) + assert len(sessions) >= 1 + + def test_session_header(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + task = store.get_task("full-test", "build-widget") + sessions = store.load_transcripts(task["transcript_files"]) + main = sessions[0] + assert main["session_id"] == "sess-main" + assert main["model"] == "gpt-5.3-codex" + assert main["depth"] == 0 + + def test_session_entries(self, harbor_job_dir): + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + task = store.get_task("full-test", "build-widget") + sessions = store.load_transcripts(task["transcript_files"]) + main = sessions[0] + assert len(main["entries"]) > 0 + assert main["entries"][0]["turn"]["kind"] == "USER_INPUT" + + def test_session_tree(self, harbor_job_dir, tmp_path): + """Test parent-child session linking.""" + store = RunStore([harbor_job_dir]) + task = store.get_task("full-test", "build-widget") + + # Add a child session transcript to the task + sessions_dir = os.path.join( + task["dir"], "agent", "serf-state", "sessions" + ) + child_entries = [ + {"kind": "header", "format_version": 1, + "session_id": "sess-child", + "parent_session_id": "sess-main", + "parent_tool_call_id": "tc-4", + "task": "Review the implementation", + "created_at": "2026-03-01T12:01:00Z", + "model": "gpt-5.3-codex", + "profile_id": "openai", "depth": 1}, + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 0, "turn": { + "kind": "USER_INPUT", + "message": {"role": "user", "content": [ + {"kind": "text", "text": "Review this work."} + ]}, + }}, + ] + child_path = os.path.join(sessions_dir, "sess-child.transcript.jsonl") + with open(child_path, "w") as f: + for e in child_entries: + f.write(json.dumps(e) + "\n") + + # Reload + task = store.get_task("full-test", "build-widget") + sessions = store.load_transcripts(task["transcript_files"]) + tree = store.build_session_tree(sessions) + assert len(tree) == 1 # one root session + assert tree[0]["session_id"] == "sess-main" + assert len(tree[0].get("children", [])) == 1 + assert tree[0]["children"][0]["session_id"] == "sess-child" +``` + +**Step 2: Run tests, verify they fail** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard && pytest test_data.py::TestTranscriptLoading -v +``` + +**Step 3: Implement `load_transcripts()` and `build_session_tree()`** + +Add to `RunStore` class in `data.py`: + +```python +def load_transcripts(self, transcript_files: list[str]) -> list[dict]: + """Parse transcript JSONL files into session dicts.""" + sessions = [] + for path in transcript_files: + session = self._parse_transcript(path) + if session: + sessions.append(session) + sessions.sort(key=lambda s: s.get("created_at", "")) + return sessions + +def _parse_transcript(self, path: str) -> dict | None: + """Parse a single transcript JSONL file.""" + header = {} + entries = [] + try: + with open(path) as f: + for line in f: + line = line.strip() + if not line: + continue + obj = json.loads(line) + if obj.get("kind") == "header": + header = obj + elif obj.get("kind") == "entry": + entries.append(obj) + except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError): + return None + + if not header: + return None + + return { + "session_id": header.get("session_id", ""), + "parent_session_id": header.get("parent_session_id", ""), + "parent_tool_call_id": header.get("parent_tool_call_id", ""), + "task": header.get("task", ""), + "created_at": header.get("created_at", ""), + "model": header.get("model", ""), + "depth": header.get("depth", 0), + "build_version": header.get("build_version", ""), + "entries": entries, + } + +def build_session_tree(self, sessions: list[dict]) -> list[dict]: + """Organize flat sessions into a parent-child tree.""" + by_id = {s["session_id"]: s for s in sessions} + + for s in sessions: + s["children"] = [] + + roots = [] + for s in sessions: + parent_id = s.get("parent_session_id", "") + if parent_id and parent_id in by_id: + by_id[parent_id]["children"].append(s) + else: + roots.append(s) + + return roots +``` + +**Step 4: Run tests, verify they pass** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard && pytest test_data.py -v +``` + +**Step 5: Commit** + +```bash +git add build_trajectory(sessions[0]) + + for r in trajectory: + assert "entries" in r + assert len(r["entries"]) > 0 + + def test_short_session(self): + """A session with only a user input and one assistant + {"kind": "entry", "seq": 1, "turn": { + "kind": "ASSISTANT", + "message": {"role": "assistant", "content": [ + {"kind": "text", "text": "I'll plan this out."}, + ]}, + }}, + ] + } + trajectory = test_trajectory.py -v +``` + +**Step 3: Implement trajectory.py** + +```python +"""Parse transcripts into high-level trajectory timelines. + +A trajectory is a list of "rounds" — each one a high-level action the +agent took (EXPLORE, EDIT, EXEC, SPAWN, SUBMIT, REVIEW, PLAN, ERROR). +This gives a forest-level view of what happened, instead of requiring +humans to scroll through dozens of raw transcript entries. +""" + +# Tools classified by action type. +EXPLORE_TOOLS = {"read_file", "glob", "grep"} +EDIT_TOOLS = {"apply_patch", "edit_file", "write_file"} +EXEC_TOOLS = {"shell"} +SUBMIT_TOOLS = {"communicate", "submit_result"} +SPAWN_TOOLS = {"spawn_agent"} +REVIEW_TOOLS = {"approve", "reject"} + +# Priority order for classification when a round has mixed tool types. +# Higher priority wins. +ACTION_PRIORITY = { + "SUBMIT": 6, + "SPAWN": 5, + "EDIT": 4, + "EXEC": 3, + "EXPLORE": 2, + "PLAN": 1, + "ERROR": 0, +} + + +def classify_round(tool_names: list[str], has_text: bool) -> str: + """Classify a round by its dominant tool type.""" + if not tool_names: + return "PLAN" if has_text else "ERROR" + + actions = set() + for name in tool_names: + if name in SUBMIT_TOOLS: + actions.add("SUBMIT") + elif name in SPAWN_TOOLS: + actions.add("SPAWN") + elif name in EDIT_TOOLS: + actions.add("EDIT") + elif name in EXEC_TOOLS: + actions.add("EXEC") + elif name in EXPLORE_TOOLS: + actions.add("EXPLORE") + elif name in REVIEW_TOOLS: + actions.add("SUBMIT") # Review tools are verdict actions + + if not actions: + return "PLAN" if has_text else "EXEC" # Unknown tools → EXEC + + return max(actions, key=lambda a: ACTION_PRIORITY.get(a, 0)) + + +def _summarize_round(action: str, tool_calls: list[dict], + tool_results: list[dict], + text: str) -> str: + """Generate a one-line summary for a trajectory round.""" + if action == "PLAN": + # Show first ~80 chars of assistant text + preview = text.strip().replace("\n", " ")[:80] + return f'"{preview}{"..." if len(text.strip()) > 80 else ""}' + + if action == "SUBMIT": + for tc in tool_calls: + if tc.get("name") in SUBMIT_TOOLS: + try: + args = tc.get("arguments", "{}") + if isinstance(args, str): + args = __import__("json").loads(args) + result_text = args.get("result", "")[:80] + return f'{tc["name"]}("{result_text}...")' + except Exception: + return tc.get("name", "submit") + return "submit" + + if action == "SPAWN": + for tc in tool_calls: + if tc.get("name") == "spawn_agent": + try: + args = tc.get("arguments", "{}") + if isinstance(args, str): + args = __import__("json").loads(args) + agent = args.get("agent", "") + task_preview = args.get("task", "")[:60] + return f'{agent}: "{task_preview}..."' + except Exception: + return "spawn_agent" + return "spawn_agent" + + if action == "EXEC": + commands = [] + for tc in tool_calls: + if tc.get("name") == "shell": + try: + args = tc.get("arguments", "{}") + if isinstance(args, str): + args = __import__("json").loads(args) + cmd = args.get("command", "")[:60] + commands.append(cmd) + except Exception: + commands.append("shell") + return "; ".join(commands) if commands else "shell" + + if action == "EDIT": + files = [] + for tc in tool_calls: + if tc.get("name") in EDIT_TOOLS: + try: + args = tc.get("arguments", "{}") + if isinstance(args, str): + args = __import__("json").loads(args) + path = args.get("path", args.get("file_path", "")) + if path: + files.append(path.split("/")[-1]) + except Exception: + pass + return ", ".join(files) if files else "edit" + + if action == "EXPLORE": + targets = [] + for tc in tool_calls: + try: + args = tc.get("arguments", "{}") + if isinstance(args, str): + args = __import__("json").loads(args) + path = args.get("path", args.get("pattern", "")) + if path: + targets.append(path.split("/")[-1]) + except Exception: + pass + return ", ".join(targets[:3]) if targets else "explore" + + return "" + + +def build_trajectory(session: dict) -> list[dict]: + """Parse a session's entries into a trajectory of classified rounds. + + A "round" is one assistant turn plus its tool results. The user input + at the start is skipped (it's the task description, shown elsewhere). + """ + entries = session.get("entries", []) + rounds = [] + round_num = 0 + + # Group entries into rounds: each ASSISTANT entry starts a new round. + current_assistant = None + current_tool_results = [] + buffered_entries = [] + + for entry in entries: + turn = entry.get("turn", {}) + kind = turn.get("kind", "") + + if kind == "USER_INPUT": + # Skip the initial task input + continue + + if kind == "STEERING": + continue + + if kind == "ASSISTANT": + # Flush previous round + if current_assistant is not None: + round_num += 1 + r = _make_round(round_num, current_assistant, + current_tool_results, buffered_entries) + rounds.append(r) + + current_assistant = entry + current_tool_results = [] + buffered_entries = [entry] + + elif kind == "TOOL_RESULTS": + current_tool_results.append(entry) + buffered_entries.append(entry) + + elif kind in ("CHECKPOINT", "SUMMARY"): + buffered_entries.append(entry) + + # Flush last round + if current_assistant is not None: + round_num += 1 + r = _make_round(round_num, current_assistant, + current_tool_results, buffered_entries) + rounds.append(r) + + return rounds + + +def _make_round(round_num: int, assistant_entry: dict, + tool_result_entries: list[dict], + raw_entries: list[dict]) -> dict: + """Build a trajectory round from an assistant turn + its results.""" + turn = assistant_entry.get("turn", {}) + msg = turn.get("message", {}) + content = msg.get("content", []) + usage = turn.get("usage", {}) + + # Extract tool calls and text + tool_calls = [] + text_parts = [] + for c in content: + if c.get("kind") == "tool_call": + tool_calls.append(c.get("tool_call", {})) + elif c.get("kind") == "text": + t = c.get("text", "").strip() + if t: + text_parts.append(t) + + # Extract tool results + tool_results = [] + for tr_entry in tool_result_entries: + for c in tr_entry.get("turn", {}).get("message", {}).get("content", []): + if c.get("kind") == "tool_result": + tool_results.append(c.get("tool_result", {})) + + tool_names = [tc.get("name", "") for tc in tool_calls] + has_text = bool(text_parts) + + action = classify_round(tool_names, has_text) + text = "\n".join(text_parts) + summary = _summarize_round(action, tool_calls, tool_results, text) + + return { + "round": round_num, + "action": action, + "summary": summary, + "tool_calls": tool_calls, + "tool_results": tool_results, + "text": text, + "usage": usage, + "entries": raw_entries, + } +``` + +**Step 4: Run tests, verify they pass** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard && pytest test_trajectory.py -v +``` + +**Step 5: Commit** + +```bash +git add tools/dashboard/trajectory.py tools/dashboard/test_trajectory.py +git commit -m "feat(dashboard): trajectory parser — classify rounds by action" +``` + +--- + +### Task 5: Markdown renderer + +Render dashboard views as markdown. +**Step 3: Implement markdown_render.py** + +```python +"""Render dashboard views as markdown. + +Markdown is the default response format. Every endpoint returns markdown +unless the client sends Accept: application/json. +""" + + +def render_run_list(runs: list[dict]) -> str: + """Render the dashboard overview as a markdown table.""" + if not runs: + return "# Eval Dashboard\n\nNo runs found.\n" + + lines = ["# Eval Dashboard", ""] + lines.append( + "| Run | Model | Pass Rate | Status | Git SHA |" + ) + lines.append( + "|-----|-------|-----------|--------|---------|" + ) + for r in runs: + total = r.get("completed", r.get("task_count", 0)) + pass_count = r.get("pass_count", 0) + pct = f"{pass_count/total*100:.0f}%" if total > 0 else "-" + rate = f"{pass_count}/{total} ({pct})" + status = r.get("status", "?") + if status == "running": + done = r.get("completed", 0) + of = r.get("task_count", 0) + status = f"running ({done}/{of})" + lines.append( + f"| {r['job_name']} | {r.get('model', '')} " + f"| {rate} | {status} | {r.get('git_sha', '')[:7]} |" + ) + lines.append("") + return "\n".join(lines) + + +def render_run_detail(run: dict, tasks: list[dict]) -> str: + """Render a single run's detail page as markdown.""" + lines = [f"# {run['job_name']}", ""] + + # Summary + total = run.get("task_count", 0) + passed = run.get("pass_count", 0) + pct = f"{passed/total*100:.0f}%" if total > 0 else "-" + lines.append(f"**Pass rate:** {passed}/{total} ({pct})") + lines.append(f"**Model:** {run.get('model', '?')}") + lines.append(f"**Git SHA:** {run.get('git_sha', '?')}") + lines.append(f"**Status:** {run.get('status', '?')}") + + # Failure breakdown + fc = run.get("failure_categories", {}) + if fc: + lines.append("") + lines.append("**Failures:**") + for cat, count in sorted(fc.items()): + lines.append(f"- {cat}: {count}") + + # Task table + lines.extend(["", "## Tasks", ""]) + lines.append("| Task | Result | Category | Sessions |") + lines.append("|------|--------|----------|----------|") + for t in sorted(tasks, key=lambda t: (t["passed"], t["name"])): + result = "PASS" if t["passed"] else "FAIL" + cat = t.get("failure_category", "") or "" + lines.append( + f"| {t['name']} | {result} | {cat} | {t.get('session_count', 0)} |" + ) + lines.append("") + return "\n".join(lines) + + +def render_task_detail(task_name: str, job_name: str, + reward: float | None, + failure_category: str, + trajectory: list[dict], + verifier_output: str = "", + reviewer_verdict: str = "") -> str: + """Render a task detail page with trajectory as markdown.""" + result = "PASSED" if reward and reward > 0 else "FAILED" + lines = [ + f"# {task_name} — {job_name}", + f"**Result:** {result}", + ] + if failure_category: + lines.append(f"**Failure category:** {failure_category}") + + # Trajectory + lines.extend(["", "## Trajectory", ""]) + if not trajectory: + lines.append("No trajectory data.") + else: + for r in trajectory: + pad = str(r["round"]).rjust(3) + action = r["action"].ljust(7) + lines.append(f" {pad} {action} {r['summary']}") + + # Verifier output + if verifier_output: + lines.extend([ + "", "## Verifier Output", "", + "```", verifier_output.strip()[-2000:], "```", + ]) + + # Reviewer verdict + if reviewer_verdict: + lines.extend([ + "", "## Reviewer Verdict", "", + reviewer_verdict, + ]) + + lines.append("") + return "\n".join(lines) +``` + +**Step 4: Run tests, verify they pass** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard && pytest test_markdown.py -v +``` + +**Step 5: Commit** + +```bash +git add tools/dashboard/markdown_render.py tools/dashboard/test_markdown.py +git commit -m "feat(dashboard): markdown renderer for all views" +``` + +--- + +### Task 6: API routes with content negotiation + +Wire up all endpoints. _wants_json(request): + return JSONResponse({"error": "not found"}, status_code=404) + return _md_response( + f"# Not Found\n\n`{task_name}` not found in `{job_name}`.\n" + ) + + # Build trajectory from transcripts + sessions = store.load_transcripts(task["transcript_files"]) + tree = store.build_session_tree(sessions) + + trajectories = [] + for root_session in tree: + trajectories.append({ + "session_id": root_session["session_id"], + "model": root_session["model"], + "depth": root_session["depth"], + "trajectory": build_trajectory(root_session), + "children": [ + { + "session_id": child["session_id"], + "task": child.get("task", ""), + "depth": child["depth"], + "trajectory": build_trajectory(child), + } + for child in root_session.get("children", []) + ], + }) + + if _wants_json(request): + task["trajectory"] = trajectories + # Remove non-serializable fields + task.pop("dir", None) + task.pop("transcript_files", None) + return JSONResponse(task) + + # Build main trajectory for markdown + main_trajectory = trajectories[0]["trajectory"] if trajectories else [] + return _md_response(render_task_detail( + task_name=task_name, + job_name=job_name, + reward=task.get("reward"), + failure_category=task.get("failure_category", ""), + trajectory=main_trajectory, + verifier_output=task.get("verifier_output", ""), + )) + + +# --- SPA catch-all --- + +@app.get("/") +def index(): + """Serve the SPA shell.""" + static_dir = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "static") + index_path = os.path.join(static_dir, "index.html") + if os.path.isfile(index_path): + return PlainTextResponse( + open(index_path).read(), media_type="text/html" + ) + return PlainTextResponse("Dashboard not built yet.", media_type="text/html") + + +# Serve static files +static_dir = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), "static") +if os.path.isdir(static_dir): + app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory=static_dir), name="static") +``` + +**Step 4: Run tests, verify they pass** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard && pytest test_server.py -v +``` + +**Step 5: Commit** + +```bash +git add tools/dashboard/server.py tools/dashboard/test_server.py +git commit -m "feat(dashboard): API routes with markdown-first content negotiation" +``` + +--- + +### Task 7: Frontend — SPA shell, dashboard page, run detail + +Build the vanilla JS frontend with client-side routing. The dashboard page shows all +runs; clicking a run shows its task grid. + +**Files:** +- Modify: `tools/dashboard/static/index.html` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/static/style.css` +- Create: `tools/dashboard/static/app.js` + +**Step 1: Implement index.html** + +The SPA shell. Clean, warm-neutral design. Loads style.css and app.js. + +See `docs/plans/2026-03-01-eval-dashboard-design.md` for the visual design spec: +- Background `#F8F8F6`, cards `#FFFFFF` with subtle borders +- System font stack for body, monospace for code +- Pass `#18A34A`, Fail `#DC2626`, Timeout `#CA8A04`, No Submit `#9CA3AF` +- 8px spacing grid, 14px body text, generous whitespace + +The HTML should include: +- A `
` with breadcrumb navigation +- A `
` container for page content +- Links to style.css and app.js + +**Step 2: Implement style.css** + +Follow the warm neutral design spec exactly. Key elements: +- Card component (`.card`) — white background, subtle border, slight shadow +- Table styles — clean rows, hover highlight, no heavy borders +- Status badges — small colored dots, not pills +- Responsive layout — works on 13" laptop and 27" monitor +- Stat cards — for pass rate, failure breakdown +- Filter bar — for result/category filtering + +**Step 3: Implement app.js** + +Client-side SPA with hash-based routing. All data fetched via the API with +`Accept: application/json`. Minimum viable pages: + +```javascript +// Routes: +// #/ → dashboard (run list) +// #/runs/{name} → run detail (task grid) +// #/runs/{name}/tasks/{task} → task detail (trajectory view) +``` + +**Dashboard page (`renderDashboard`):** +- Fetch `GET /api/runs` with JSON accept header +- Render as a table: run name (link), model, pass rate bar, status, git SHA +- Running runs pinned to top with live progress + +**Run detail page (`renderRunDetail`):** +- Fetch `GET /api/runs/{name}` with JSON accept header +- Stat cards: pass count, fail count, failure category breakdown +- Task grid: sortable table with name, result badge, category, session count +- Filter buttons: All, Pass, Fail, Timeout, Wrong Answer, No Submit + +**Step 4: Verify manually** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard +DASHBOARD_DATA_DIRS=/tmp/full-mk6 uvicorn server:app --reload --port 8080 +# Open http://localhost:8080 in browser +``` + +Verify: dashboard loads, shows runs, clicking a run shows tasks. + +**Step 5: Commit** + +```bash +git add tools/dashboard/static/ +git commit -m "feat(dashboard): frontend — dashboard and run detail pages" +``` + +--- + +### Task 8: Frontend — task detail with trajectory view + +The core feature. Renders the trajectory timeline with drill-down into raw entries +and a context panel for verifier output. + +**Files:** +- Modify: `tools/dashboard/static/app.js` +- Modify: `tools/dashboard/static/style.css` + +**Step 1: Implement trajectory timeline rendering** + +Fetch `GET /api/runs/{name}/tasks/{task}` with JSON accept header. Render: + +**Left panel (trajectory):** +- Vertical timeline with dots and connecting line (like git log) +- Each round: round number, action badge (colored dot), summary text +- Action colors: EXPLORE=blue, EDIT=yellow, EXEC=gray, SPAWN=purple, + SUBMIT=green, REVIEW=red, PLAN=muted, ERROR=red +- Click round to expand raw entries below it (tool args, results, usage) +- Subagent sessions shown indented inline at their spawn point + +**Right panel (context):** +- Task instruction (from the USER_INPUT entry) +- Verifier test output +- Failure category +- Reward + +**Step 2: Implement expand/collapse for raw entries** + +Each round is clickable. When expanded, show: +- Tool call name + arguments (collapsible if > 300 chars) +- Tool result content (collapsible if > 400 chars) +- Usage stats (input/output tokens) + +Use the same truncation and expand logic as the existing transcript-viewer.py +but with the warm neutral styling. + +**Step 3: Implement subagent session inlining** + +When a round has action=SPAWN, check if the response includes child sessions. +Render the child session's trajectory indented beneath the spawn round, with a +visual connector showing it's a nested session. Include the child's verdict +(APPROVED/REJECTED) if it's a reviewer. + +**Step 4: Verify manually** + +```bash +cd tools/dashboard +DASHBOARD_DATA_DIRS=/tmp/full-mk6 uvicorn server:app --reload --port 8080 +# Open http://localhost:8080, navigate to a task, verify trajectory view +``` + +Verify: +- Trajectory shows labeled rounds +- Clicking expands raw entries +- Context panel shows verifier output +- Subagent sessions appear inline + +**Step 5: Commit** + +```bash +git add tools/dashboard/static/ +git commit -m "feat(dashboard): trajectory view with drill-down and context panel" +``` + +--- + +### Task 9: Deploy to magic-kingdom + +Deploy the dashboard as a persistent service on magic-kingdom. + +**Files:** +- Create: `tools/dashboard/requirements.txt` +- Modify: `tools/dashboard/server.py` (add CLI args) + +**Step 1: Create requirements.txt** + +``` +fastapi>=0.115 +uvicorn>=0.30 +``` + +**Step 2: Add CLI argument parsing to server.py** + +Add an `if __name__ == "__main__":` block that accepts `--data-dir` (repeatable), +`--host`, and `--port` arguments. Pass data dirs to `RunStore`. Run uvicorn. + +```python +if __name__ == "__main__": + import argparse + parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Serf Eval Dashboard") + parser.add_argument("--data-dir", action="append", default=[], + help="Directory to scan for eval runs (repeatable)") + parser.add_argument("--host", default="0.0.0.0") + parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=8080) + args = parser.parse_args() + + if args.data_dir: + global store + store = RunStore(args.data_dir) + + import uvicorn + uvicorn.run(app, host=args.host, port=args.port) +``` + +**Step 3: Deploy** + +```bash +# Copy dashboard to magic-kingdom +rsync -avz tools/dashboard/ jesse@magic-kingdom:~/git/eval-dashboard/ + +# Install deps +ssh jesse@magic-kingdom 'cd ~/git/eval-dashboard && pip install -r requirements.txt' + +# Launch in screen session +ssh jesse@magic-kingdom 'screen -dmS dashboard python3 ~/git/eval-dashboard/server.py \ + --data-dir /tmp/full-mk6 \ + --data-dir /tmp/full-mk4 \ + --data-dir /tmp/full-mk3 \ + --port 8080' +``` + +**Step 4: Verify** + +```bash +# Health check +curl http://magic-kingdom:8080/health + +# Markdown API +curl http://magic-kingdom:8080/api/runs + +# JSON API +curl -H "Accept: application/json" http://magic-kingdom:8080/api/runs + +# Open in browser +open http://magic-kingdom:8080 +``` + +**Step 5: Commit** + +```bash +git add tools/dashboard/requirements.txt +git commit -m "feat(dashboard): CLI args and requirements for deployment" +``` + +--- + +## Phase 2 (future, not part of this plan) + +- **Comparison views:** `/compare` for cross-run diff, `/tasks/{task}` for task history +- **Multi-trial support:** Per-task pass rates across reps, trajectory comparison +- **Cross-harness comparison:** serf vs codex on same tasks +- **Auto-refresh:** Poll for new results on running evals +- **Agent workflow integration:** Agent dispatches investigation tasks via dashboard API +- **Write eval data to /data/ directly** (Task #50)
